diagrams.title: Euler's Theorem Formula Structure
diagrams.description: Shows the relationship between homogeneous function f(x,y) and its partial derivatives
elements: Partial derivative with respect to x: ∂f/∂x
Partial derivative with respect to y: ∂f/∂y
diagrams.purpose: Visualize the key formula and understand how variables multiply with their partial derivatives

diagrams.title: Homogeneity Check Flowchart
diagrams.description: Step-by-step process to determine if a function is homogeneous and find its degree
elements: Step 3: Check if t^n can be factored out
Step 4: If yes, degree = n; if no, not homogeneous
diagrams.purpose: Learn systematic approach to check homogeneity

diagrams.title: Euler's Theorem Extended to n Variables
diagrams.description: Visual representation of Euler's theorem for multiple variables
diagrams.purpose: Understand generalization of Euler's theorem beyond two variables

diagrams.title: Degree of Homogeneity Examples
diagrams.description: Visual comparison of functions with different degrees
elements: Degree 2: f(x,y) = x² + xy + y² (area-like scaling)
diagrams.purpose: Intuitive understanding of what different degrees mean visually
